pattern: Sliding window with a character-count map keeps the window valid while growing it greedily to maximise length.
hints: A window stays valid as long as no character appears more than twice. What does adding a new character do to the window?
When a character would appear a third time you must shrink the window from the left until it appears at most twice again.
Maintain a frequency map and two pointers; the answer is the maximum window size ever seen while it remained valid.

edge_inputs.description: two characters alternating, whole string valid
no character repeats at all, whole string valid
three consecutive same chars then different
minimum length string, two same chars
minimum length string, two different chars
answer spans middle not beginning or end
valid window resets multiple times, pick the best

stress.description: random lowercase string of length 100 to stress-test window correctness
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    import string
    n = max(2, size)
    s = ''.join(rng.choice(string.ascii_lowercase) for _ in range(n))
    return [s]
